# opjmath oracle vectors

`intmath.txt` holds edge-case input/output vectors for every function in
`internal/opjmath` (ported from `opj_intmath.h`), generated by running the C
functions from the OpenJPEG reference build.

## Format

One test per line: `<name> <arg...> <result>`, whitespace separated, decimal.
Signed 32-bit values print as `%d`, unsigned 32-bit as `%u`, 64-bit signed as
`%lld`, unsigned 64-bit as `%llu`. The Go test (`intmath_test.go`) knows the
arg/result types for each `name` and parses accordingly.

Function names: `int_min`, `int_max`, `uint_min`, `uint_max`, `uint_adds`,
`uint_subs`, `int_clamp`, `int64_clamp`, `int_abs`, `int_ceildiv`,
`uint_ceildiv`, `uint64_ceildiv_res_uint32`, `int_ceildivpow2`,
`int64_ceildivpow2`, `uint_ceildivpow2`, `int_floordivpow2`,
`uint_floordivpow2`, `int_floorlog2`, `uint_floorlog2`, `int_fix_mul`,
`int_fix_mul_t1`, `int_add_no_overflow`, `int_sub_no_overflow`.

The input sweep covers INT32_MIN/MAX, powers of two ±1, small negatives, zero,
and (for the fixed-point multiplies) products that overflow int32 — exercising
the two's-complement wraparound of the release build.

## Regeneration

Built with `-DNDEBUG` so the C debug asserts in `opj_int_fix_mul` /
`opj_int_fix_mul_t1` are disabled, matching the release semantics the Go port
reproduces (the `>>` result is truncated to int32 rather than asserted).

```
gcc -O2 -DNDEBUG \
    -I oracle/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2 \
    -I oracle/openjpeg/build/src/lib/openjp2 \
    oracle/harness/w1/opjmath_vectors.c \
    oracle/openjpeg/build/bin/libopenjp2.a -lm -lpthread -o /tmp/opjmath_vectors
/tmp/opjmath_vectors > testdata/vectors/opjmath/intmath.txt
```
